channel.title: commit on Delnegend's Cave
channel.description: Recent content in commit on Delnegend's Cave

item.title: Clearing old commits from a repository
item.description: Use case A repository that hosts a static website. Steps Creating a new branch 1 git checkout -b <new_branch_name> Adding files to the new branch 1 2 git add -A git commit -m "Initial commit" Deleting the old branch 1 git branch -D <old_branch_name> Renaming the new branch 1 git branch -m <new_branch_name> References How can I delete old commits in Github via terminal? | Usama Baig, zx485

item.title: How to automatically sign commits with SSH key
item.description: Generating a new SSH key Read this to learn how to generate a public/private key pair. Adding the SSH key to your GitHub account Profile > Settings > SSH and GPG keys > New SSH key Enter a title of your choice and the public key. Change key type to Signing key and click Add SSH key. Configuring Git to sign commits by default 1 2 3 git config --global commit.
